barrier.text: The D/J/C publisher is the publication component.  Its journal is the publication point: a ciphertext tail with no durable mapping is unreachable after recovery.  Conversely, a mapping is written only after its ciphertext range has crossed

X6/O3 answer strict-path practicality without upgrading crash claims.  X6 changes marker/checkpoint publication from filesystem-wide \texttt{syncfs} to marker-file \texttt{fsync} (1,070 retained A2 calls), leaving data and journal barriers.

Strict publication still drains coalesced writes before sync returns.  It updates sidecar dirty epochs and D/J append cutpoints at batch boundaries while recording per-block ciphertext offsets before journal mapping preparation.  When no ex

AEGIS-Q fixes 4~KiB authenticated records and coalesces larger writes into bounded 512~KiB batches; 16/64/128~KiB chunks change amortization, not nonce/AAD format.  The remaining strict cost is barrier publication.
